market_context: No news items in today's data pull; assessment based on 30-day price bars only. JNJ catalysts from 2026-06-10 (MHRA nipocalimab authorization, Firefly Bio acquisition, Goldman healthcare conference) remain unfalsified with continued price strength — re-issuing buy. GOOGL technically oversold (RSI 28, -8.7% over 10 sessions) but high-volume selling on down days signals trend continuation risk, not a bottom. MSFT -13.7% over 8 sessions with no confirmation of support. All other watchlist names default to hold pending fresh news or fundamental data.

signals.thesis: Catalysts from 2026-06-10 signals remain unfalsified: MHRA nipocalimab authorization, $1B Firefly Bio acquisition, Goldman healthcare conference positive commentary. Price recovered from $222.91 trough (2026-06-02) to $238.52 over 6 sessions, confirming momentum. Thesis falsified if price reverses below $230 or adverse talc-litigation news materializes.

signals.thesis: 30-day gain of +7.9% followed by an RSI dip to 40 indicates a pause rather than a reversal, but no fresh news or fundamentals today to upgrade to a buy. Hold until a catalyst or valuation basis is available.

Down -13.7% from 2026-06-01 high of $460.48 to $397.41 over 8 sessions with sustained volume; prior digest cited Xbox layoffs and macro rate fears. No bottom signal confirmed; entering into a high-velocity decline without a catalyst is not justified.

signals.thesis: RSI of 32.9 and -4.27% over 30 days places NVDA near oversold territory, but prior context (China-Taiwan tensions, semiconductor supply-chain risk) remains unresolved. No new confirming catalyst to buy the dip today.

RSI 28 is technically oversold after -8.7% over 10 sessions (from $390.15 to $356.33), but June 2-3 volume spike (1.8M and 1.6M) on down days indicates institutional selling rather than a capitulation bottom. Insufficient basis to buy into the decline without a fundamental catalyst.

+9.93% over 30 days and RSI 66.6 show strong momentum, but no fundamental basis to add a healthcare position on top of the pending JNJ buy. Prior signals correctly noted analyst PT raises are not a this-desk catalyst.
